fix: delegate model selection to manager in api_proxy

The anyclaw-server should not specify which model to use when
forwarding requests to anyclaw-manager. The manager is responsible
for model selection based on its configured channels.

This allows centralized model management across all instances.

fix: create ReplyDispatcher for proper agent reply via toq

dispatchReplyFromConfig requires a ReplyDispatcher that handles
sending the agent's response. Created one using
createReplyDispatcherWithTyping with a deliver function that
sends replies back to the remote agent via the toq SDK.

Fix: transformer uses simple TITLE|||/CONTENT format instead of JSON

Same fix as image prompt-gen: MiniMax reasoning model breaks JSON.
Changed to pipe-delimited format with ---CONTENT--- separator.
3-layer fallback: simple format → JSON → raw text extraction.
Also truncates input to prevent token overflow.

fix(skill): install cortex-memory skill to ~/skills/ instead of ~/.openclaw/skills/

OpenClaw resolves global skills from ~/skills/ (home directory level).
The previous path caused the skill to be invisible to agents despite
being installed.

fix: 全局命令改为本目录 openclaw.cmd + PowerShell 写入用户 PATH

不再遍历 PATH 找可写目录（各种边界问题）。

## Summary

Fix the Azure OpenAI regression reported in #13499.

Dated Azure API versions such as `2024-12-01-preview` are intended to
stay on Cherry's chat / deployment-based path, while only generic
`preview` / `v1` should use the Responses API. The regression was that
aiCore's internal `azure` provider still defaulted to the Responses
transport, so deployment-based Azure requests could still generate
`/openai/deployments/<deploym

filtering, the plugin dispatches our own outbound messages back
to the agent, creating a loop. Now fetches local address on
startup and skips messages from self.

fix(chromium): swap ports so CDP proxy owns 9222, eliminating headless bypass

The CDP Service is headless (ClusterIP: None), so kube-proxy does not
apply port/targetPort mapping. DNS resolves to pod IPs directly,
meaning connections to port 9222 hit browserless instead of the nginx
CDP proxy on 9223. This bypasses all Chrome launch arg injection
(anti-bot flags, extraArgs).

Fix: move browserless to internal port 9224 and give the CDP proxy
port 9222. Every connection to the standard CDP port n

fix: reduce GPT-5.4 maxTokens to 16384, reorder AGENTS.md sentinel steps

OpenClaw ignores per-model maxTokens and uses an internal default (~32000),
causing 400 errors on models with 16384 cap (gpt-5-mini, possibly gpt-5.4).
Fix: set all model maxTokens to 16384 max.

Also added stdout capture requirement: pipe Copilot output to file with tee
to prevent research results loss in background mode.

installs deps, builds, deletes .gitignore) then immediately ran
provision-vendor-patched.sh (which requires pristine vendor state).

git clone + checkout without modifying the working tree. CI validation
jobs use this flag; build jobs continue using the full setup.

fix(gateway): SSO config not visible on login page after saving settings

oauth2Config was loaded once at startup with `const` and never refreshed.
When admin saved SSO settings via the UI, values were persisted to DB but
the in-memory config remained null, so `/api/sso/config` always returned
`{ enabled: false }` and the login page never showed the SSO button.

Replace the one-shot load with an in-memory cache (`cachedOAuth2Config` /
`cachedSsoEnabled`) that is:
- populated from DB at startup
-

fix(gateway-rpc): add device identity auth to WS connect handshake

MC was authenticating with only the gateway bearer token, which grants
no operator scopes. This caused sessions.list, config.get, and other
scope-gated RPC methods to fail with "missing scope: operator.read".

Now loads the CLI device identity (~/.openclaw/identity/device.json),
signs the connect payload with the ed25519 private key, and sends the
device object in the WS connect handshake — matching how the CLI
authenticates. Al

- ImageContent → {"type": "image", "source_type": "base64"

feat(sandbox): add retry logic for transient container failures (#1232)

* feat(sandbox): add retry logic for transient container failures (#1224)

SandboxManager::execute_with_policy() had no retry logic. Transient Docker
errors (daemon temporarily unavailable, container creation race conditions,
container start failures) caused immediate job failure.

Adds up to 2 retries (3 total attempts) with exponential backoff (2s, 4s)
for transient error types only:
- DockerNotAvailable
- ContainerCreati

fix(llm): persist refreshed Anthropic OAuth token after Keychain re-read (#1213)

* fix(llm): persist refreshed Anthropic OAuth token after Keychain re-read (#1136)

The Anthropic OAuth provider stored its token as an immutable SecretString.
When a 401 triggered a Keychain re-read, the fresh token was used for a
single retry but never persisted — every subsequent request reused the
expired original token, causing repeated auth failures.

Changes:
- Wrap token in RwLock<SecretString> so it can be

- HEARTBEAT_TIMEZONE=Region/City — IANA timezone name for fire_at (e.g.

- When fire_at is set, interval_secs is ignored

Tier-aware exec-approvals dir permissions: 750 at Tier 0-1, 770 at Tier 2+

Tier 0-1 already has exec-approvals.json deployed by the entrypoint, so
the directory only needs traverse+read (750). Tier 2+ deletes the file
(full exec mode) but the gateway creates it at runtime for state
tracking, so needs write access (770). Previous commit set 770
unconditionally which was overly permissive for lower tiers.

feat: Reuse Codex CLI OAuth tokens for ChatGPT backend LLM calls (#693)

* feat: add Codex auth.json token reuse for LLM authentication

When LLM_USE_CODEX_AUTH=true, IronClaw reads the Codex CLI's auth.json
(default ~/.codex/auth.json) and extracts the API key or OAuth access
token. This lets IronClaw piggyback on a Codex login without
implementing its own OAuth flow.

New env vars:
  - LLM_USE_CODEX_AUTH: enable Codex auth fallback (default: false)
  - CODEX_AUTH_PATH: override path to auth.js

### What this PR does

Before this PR:
- Zed editor could not open directories because it used the same VSCode
URL scheme (`vscode://file/path?windowId=_blank`), which Zed does not
recognize.
- The "Open" button always showed the first detected editor's icon
regardless of which editor the user selected from the dropdown.
- The button displayed a text label ("Open") and used an ellipsis icon
(···) for the dropdown trigge

feat: update clawpage skill description

Fix exec-approvals dir permissions at Tier 2+ — gateway needs write access

At Tier 2+ the exec-approvals file is deleted (full exec mode), but the
gateway still tries to create/manage exec-approvals.json at runtime for
state tracking. The directory was left root:root after mkdir, blocking
the gateway (running as openclaw) from writing. Set dir to root:openclaw
770 unconditionally so the gateway can create files at any tier.

fix(provider): sync providers_config after creating new provider (#6388)

Fixes #6283

When adding a new embedding provider, the knowledge base creation page
did not show the new provider until restart.

Root cause: create_provider() did not update self.providers_config,
which is used by get_provider_config_list() to return provider lists.

This fix syncs the in-memory config after loading the new provider,
consistent with how reload() handles config updates.

fix: prevent trivial conversations from overwriting meaningful state

Short conversations (< 2 real user messages) no longer overwrite
existing meaningful state. This prevents the "recovery death spiral"
where:
1. Secret told → state saved ✅
2. /new → state injected → model ignores it → "I don't remember"
3. agent_end overwrites good state with "I don't remember" ❌
4. All subsequent recoveries fail permanently

Now step 3 is blocked: a 1-message "what was my secret?" conversation
won't overwrite
v21 civilization kernel cycle

Fix alternating online/offline on app restart

Root cause: sidecar not killed on exit → port occupied → new sidecar
on different port → pair page only checked one port.

Fixes:
- Kill sidecar on RunEvent::Exit (not just tray Quit)
- 500ms delay after kill to let port release
- Pair/office pages try ports [9090, 9091, 9099] as fallback
- Also pass HOME env var to sidecar

fix: persist CLI token counts to session DB for /insights

Token usage was tracked in-memory during CLI sessions (session_prompt_tokens,
session_completion_tokens) but never written to the SQLite session DB. The
gateway persisted tokens via session_store.update_session(), but CLI sessions
always showed 0 tokens in /insights.

Now run_agent.py persists token deltas to the DB after each API call for CLI
sessions. Gateway sessions continue to use their existing persist path to
avoid double-counting

fix: always update state on agent_end, archive previous state

The agent_end hook was skipping state extraction when CURRENT_STATE.md
already contained meaningful content. This meant once a state was written,
subsequent conversations never updated it — the next /new would always
recover stale data.

Now: every session end overwrites CURRENT_STATE.md with the latest
conversation state. Previous state is archived to STATE_ARCHIVE_<ts>.md.

Fix stale daily usage: vault tracks usage_date, proxy discards old-date values

back under today's timestamp. Next proxy restart loaded those stale values
and re-sent them, forming an infinite feedback loop.

- Include usage_date in /api/keys response
- SyncFromVault: treat vault usage as 0 if

feat(streaming): replace fixed timeout with resettable idle timeout (#13497)

### What this PR does

Before this PR:

The streaming timeout used a fixed `AbortSignal.timeout()` that starts
counting from the initial request. For long-running SSE streams (e.g.,
complex reasoning or tool-calling chains), the request could be aborted
even while data was actively flowing.

After this PR:

Introduces an `IdleTimeoutController` that resets its countdown every
time a stream chunk is received. The timeou

Root cause: gateway uses a two-step challenge-response protocol, not
a simple query param. Previous attempts failed because:
1. Wrong token (device-auth.json vs OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN in .env)
2. Wrong frame format (missing type:"req" field)
3. Missing required connect params: minProtocol, maxProtocol, client.mode

Protocol (reverse-engineered from openclaw dist source):
1. Server sends  { type:"event", event:"connect.challenge", payload:{nonc

Use BEL terminator for taskbar ANSI sequences

Fix stale daily key-usage counters after midnight vault reset

After the vault broadcasts usage_reset at midnight, the proxy's local
todayUsage/todayAttempts counters still held yesterday's values. The
SyncFromVault max(vault=0, local=yesterday) logic kept them alive, and
the next heartbeat pushed stale values back to the vault.

Fixes:
- KeyManager tracks lastSyncDate; SyncFromVault discards local counters
  when the date has changed (midnight rollover), so vault values win
- Add ResetDailyCoun

fix: ensure binaryPath exists before skipping plugin registration (#26)

* fix: ensure binaryPath exists before skipping plugin registration

The idempotency check in register_plugin() previously only verified
that the plugin path existed in the config. If binaryPath was missing
(e.g. from a prior install version or manual config edit), re-running
install.sh would skip registration, leaving the plugin broken.

Now both conditions must be true to skip: plugin path present AND
binaryPath configure

fix: reject probe responses without worldId for world peers (#71)

## Problem

The Gateway's active world probing passed for stale ghost worlds because
their announced endpoint (`3.17.5.202:8099`) happened to be the
Gateway's own peer listener port. The Gateway's `/peer/ping` returns `{
role: "gateway" }` with no `worldId` field, so the check `if
(data.worldId && expectedWorldId && ...)` was skipped entirely --
falling through to `return true`.

namespace.

fix: kill orphaned bash processes on terminal session cleanup

Previously, closing/switching away from the terminal tab only disposed
the frontend xterm UI without telling the backend, leaving /bin/bash
exec processes running indefinitely inside the container. Adds a
stop_terminal_session IPC command that inspects the Docker exec to find
the bash PID and sends SIGTERM before cleaning up the session.

fix(update): force app exit after quitAndInstall on macOS

On macOS, autoUpdater.quitAndInstall() closes all windows but the
window-all-closed handler does not call app.quit() (standard macOS
behavior + close-to-tray). This leaves the process alive and Squirrel
cannot finish replacing the app bundle.

- Use isSilent=true to avoid confirmation dialogs blocking install
- Call app.exit(0) after 1s delay to force process termination
- Bump version to 1.8.28 for base package testing

feat(runtime): add CancellationToken to sub-agent loop for cooperative shutdown

Thread CancellationToken through RuntimeSpawner → run_sub_agent →
sub_agent_loop. Each sub-agent receives a child token derived from
the parent runtime's shutdown token. When the daemon receives SIGTERM,
the token fires and the sub-agent loop exits cooperatively at the next
iteration boundary — without waiting for the hard timeout.

- RuntimeSpawner now stores a CancellationToken alongside runtime + outbound
- Runti

fix: add TTL-based peer expiry and active world probing (#70)

## Problem

The Gateway and World Agent had no TTL/expiry mechanism for peers. When
a world container stopped, its peer announcement persisted indefinitely
because:

1. Other DAP peers kept gossipping the stale entry back to the Gateway
2. Gateway set `lastSeen: now` on every `upsertPeer()` call, so stale
entries never aged out
3. Multiple stopped containers could share the same `host:port` endpoint
(using Docker internal port 8099),
添加代理

fix: auto-focus file preview tab when selecting file from sidebar

Move setActiveTabId calls out of setPreviewTabs updater function.
Calling a state setter inside another setter's updater is a side effect
in what React expects to be a pure function, causing the active tab
update to be suppressed. Use a ref (tabsRef) to read the latest tabs
without stale closures, and call both state setters as separate
top-level updates that React properly batches together.

feat: add incremental repatch command to avoid full recompilation

On cross-platform workflows (edit on Mac, build on Windows), the
reset+patch cycle touches all file timestamps causing ninja to
recompile everything. The new `dev.py repatch` command tracks patch
file hashes in a manifest and only resets/re-applies changed patches,
keeping unmodified source file timestamps intact for incremental builds.

which caused Gateway to route wake messages to the agent's default channel
instead of the originating channel (e.g. roundtable thread). Now extracts
the channel ID from parentSessionKey and passes `--to channel:<id>`.

feat(canvas): canvas_response push type — close the agent response loop (#1074)

Adds 'canvas_response' type to POST /canvas/push. When a canvas query
arrives via chat, the agent processes it and pushes a structured response
card back via this endpoint. The card is emitted as both canvas_push
(for SSE relay) and canvas_message (for living-canvas rendering).

This closes the loop: human types on canvas → chat message → agent →
canvas_response push → card appears on canvas.

Fix drag-and-drop: use plain variable for synchronous event checks

Root cause: Svelte 5 reactive state ($state) updates are batched
and don't propagate synchronously within the same event loop tick.
When ondragstart set dragDeptId via $state, the ondragover handler
on the target fired before the state update propagated, causing
the guard check (!dragDeptId) to return early without calling
preventDefault() — which tells the browser to reject the drop.

Fix: maintain a plain JS variable (_dragId)

fix: prevent Windows Electrobun artifact upload timeout (#996)

The Windows build produces ~285MB of uncompressed artifacts (NSIS .exe +
MSIX), causing the GitHub Actions SAS token to expire during the ~1.5hr
upload. macOS/Linux succeed because their artifacts use .tar.zst/.dmg.

Changes:
- Pre-compress Windows .exe to .zip before upload (Compress-Archive),
  typically 20-40% size reduction on NSIS installers
- Split MSIX into its own artifact upload (was doubling the upload size
  by bundling ~

Switch to CSS columns for multi-column + drag-and-drop

The JS grid approach (separate DOM containers per column) broke
drag-and-drop because events couldn't cross container boundaries.

Now uses CSS column-count on a single DOM container — all nodes
are siblings in the same tree, so drag events work across columns.
Browser handles the column flow automatically:
- 2 columns default
- 3 columns at 1400px+
- 1 column at <900px
- break-inside: avoid-column keeps rows intact

nodes to be destroyed mid-drag which cancelled the operation.

snapshot throughout the drag, then unfreeze on drop or dragend.
This prevents Svelte's reactivity from rebuilding the grid while
the user is dragging between columns.

fix: inject vault secrets into process env at startup (#509)

The credential vault (`~/.librefang/vault.enc`) was never loaded by the
CLI or daemon at startup. All code paths (kernel boot, driver creation,
doctor, config test-key) resolved API keys via `std::env::var()` which
only sees `.env` and system environment variables — vault secrets were

fix: use gatewayUrl hash param for OpenClaw UI auto-connect

OpenClaw UI reads gatewayUrl (not wsUrl) from URL params.
It checks hash params first, then search params.
Injecting #gatewayUrl=...&token=... into the hash so UI
auto-fills and auto-connects without user interaction.

Root cause of 1008 pairing required: client was connecting
without any token because wsUrl param was being ignored.

fix(agents): prevent deleting the last accessible directory (#13483)

### What this PR does

Before this PR:
Deleting the only accessible directory in Agent Settings fails silently
— `resolveAccessiblePaths()` treats `[]` the same as `undefined`,
replacing it with a default path. The directory reappears after
deletion.

After this PR:
- Backend: `AgentService.updateAgent()` and
`SessionService.updateSession()` reject empty `accessible_paths` with an
explicit error, enforcing the existing non-emp

feat: implement phase 0 kms flow for secure credential encryption (#14)

Created GCP Cloud KMS infrastructure via Terraform, including key ring, crypto key, dedicated service account, and IAM bindings. Developed Node.js Cloud Functions `encrypt` and `decrypt` to process Supabase `service_role` keys securely without exposing plaintext credentials. Deployed functions via Terraform using Cloud Functions v2 and a secure storage bucket. Implemented a test script using gcloud identity tokens for round
refactor(notification_gateway): fix return value for unknown or disabled providers

ai: add channel-isolated conversation memory

Each conversation source (shell, Feishu, Telegram, scheduler)
now tags its messages with a channel ID. ai_memory_build()
filters by channel so different sources maintain independent
conversation history in the shared ring buffer.

New API:
- ai_set_channel() / ai_get_channel() — set active channel
- ai_memory_add() — add message with channel tag
- ai_memory_build() — build messages for a specific channel
- ai_memory_clear_channel() — clear one channe

safety, quota) respond correctly with auth

fix: use system URLLoaderFactory in browser updater to prevent startup crash

OcbotBrowserUpdater was using profile->GetDefaultStoragePartition() to
get a URLLoaderFactory, which could CHECK-fail during early startup when
the profile's network context isn't fully initialized. Replaced with
SystemNetworkContextManager which is available without a Profile and
avoids the lifetime/initialization issue entirely.

storage (data_ws/AGENT_DATA_DIR). Since tools write to AGENT_DATA_DIR,
memory was invisible in system prompts after container restarts.

Now checks all 4 paths: tool_ws/memory/memory.md, tool_ws/memory.md,
data_ws/memory/memory.md, data_ws/memory.md — matching the pattern
used by soul.md and focus.md.

heartbeat: add device health check on each ping cycle

Before the LLM ping, check_device_health() samples heap
usage from the platform API. When usage exceeds 80%, a
memory warning event is posted to the heartbeat buffer,
which triggers an AI summary on the next tick.

Platform-aware: ESP-IDF uses heap_caps API, RT-Thread
uses rt_memory_info().

tools: add heap fragmentation to memory_info tool

Add largest contiguous free block and fragmentation percentage
to the ESP-IDF memory_info tool output. Fragmentation is
calculated as (1 - largest_block / free_total) * 100%.

getNextTask could return a directly-assigned cross-lane task, causing
artdirector to be nudged even when design-lane queue was empty.

different from agent's lane, treat as lane-empty → suppress.

calls. Any HTTP response (including 4xx) counts as "online";

info.kind==="final" only contains the last assistant turn's text,
causing the finalized card to lose all previous turns' content.

sent to the card during streaming) for f

fix(spawn-interceptor): reject closed sessions that predate task spawn

After a Gateway restart, the ACP poller could match new tasks to old
closed sessions because acpx reuses session names. This caused false
completion notifications — tasks reported as done when they had just
started or never ran.

Fix: check closed_at timestamp against task spawnedAt. If the session
was closed before the task was spawned, skip it during matching.
